response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack web engineer whose core competencies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) are largely orthogonal to the role's primary requirements. C++ appears only in a six-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor-thesis audio project; it is absent from the Skills section entirely. There is no evidence of multi-threaded systems design, industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850), IEC 62443 secure-development lifecycle, or any industrial automation domain experience, which are the core pillars of this position.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby — millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O and fibers)
Event-driven architecture background via the GISCollective open-source geo-data platform built on Node.js
Broad exposure to agile practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) and cross-functional collaboration across multiple companies
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — roughly six months in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis audio project; C++ does not appear in the candidate's own Skills section
Zero exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL), industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, DNP 3.0), or IoT/automation domain knowledge

Educational background (BS Computer Science + BA Photography) does not align with the required B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech engineering degree, and total experience skews heavily toward web/TypeScript rather than systems programming
